eval-kr-001.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the Device ID for the Audio controller on NVL PCD-H? EVAL: Device ID is 0xD328, BDF is 0:31:3, Vendor ID is 0x8086
eval-kr-001.description: Knowledge recall: NVL PCD-H ACE Device ID

eval-kr-002.template: use skills_fv_audio. How many DSP cores does NVL PCD-H have compared to PCH-S? EVAL: PCD-H has 4 HiFi5 HP cores plus 1 ULP core (Core 0). PCH-S has 2 HiFi5 HP cores plus 1 ULP core (Core 0) plus 1 ANNA core. SRAM is 4.5MB PCD-H vs 2.25MB PCH-S
eval-kr-002.description: Knowledge recall: NVL PCD-H vs PCH-S DSP core count

eval-kr-003.template: use skills_fv_audio. How many SoundWire segments does NVL PCD-H support and what are their special functions? EVAL: 5 segments total (SNDWSC equals 5). Seg 0 has alt iDisp-A function on PCD-H. Seg 2 and 3 support up to 4 data lanes for multi-lane amplifier arrays. Seg 4 is external on PCD-H with up to 5 data lanes. On PCH-S Seg 4 is on-die with iDisp-A and CNVi alt function
eval-kr-003.description: Knowledge recall: SoundWire segment count and functions

eval-kr-004.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the BAR assignments for the Audio controller? EVAL: BAR0 is 512KB for HDA compatible registers. BAR1 is 4KB for ACPI and PCI config. BAR2 is 2MB for the DSP domain and requires GPROCEN equals 1

eval-kr-005.template: use skills_fv_audio. What voltage does the HDA link operate at on NVL? EVAL: 1.8V only, not 3.3V. Both SDI pins operate at 1.8V for all external codecs

eval-kr-006.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the SSP/I2S configuration on NVL? EVAL: 3 SSP ports with I2SPC equals 3, bidirectional, max BCLK 12.288 MHz

eval-kr-007.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are CORB and RIRB in the HDA controller? EVAL: CORB is Command Output Ring Buffer for host to codec verbs. RIRB is Response Input Ring Buffer for codec to host responses. Both are DMA-based ring buffers in system memory

eval-kr-008.template: use skills_fv_audio. Are the Audio Device IDs the same between NVL PCD-H and PCH-S? EVAL: No. PCD-H is 0xD328 with range D328-D32F. PCH-S is 0xD228 with range D228-D22F. Different IDs for different die variants
eval-kr-008.description: Knowledge recall: PCD-H vs PCH-S Device ID difference

eval-sr-001.template: use skills_fv_audio. Is the audio controller enumerated on my NVL platform? EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/config-checkout sub-skill and does not attempt to answer from agent-level knowledge alone

eval-sr-002.template: use skills_fv_audio. No codecs detected on the HDA link, STATESTS reads 0x0000. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/hda sub-skill and references GCTL.CRST toggle and codec detection sequence

eval-sr-003.template: use skills_fv_audio. SoundWire codec on Segment 2 is not enumerating. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/soundwire sub-skill and checks SHIM LCTL and segment configuration

eval-sr-004.template: use skills_fv_audio. DSP firmware load is timing out on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/dsp sub-skill and describes checking ADSPCS register for SPA and CPA status and GPROCEN for processing enable and SRAM PGCTL for power gating

eval-sr-005.template: use skills_fv_audio. S0ix is blocked and I suspect the audio controller is the blocker. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/power sub-skill and describes checking PMCSR power state and GCTL.CRST link reset and DSP core SPA and LTR values for S0ix readiness

eval-sr-006.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio NGA test failed with StreamError, help me debug. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/failure-analysis sub-skill and references NGA failure analysis workflow and error pattern matching

eval-sd-001.template: use skills_fv_audio. Any known sightings for HDA codec detection failures on NVL? EVAL: agent delegates to HSDES skill or agent with appropriate search keywords including HDA codec detection STATESTS NVL ACE

eval-sd-002.template: use skills_fv_audio. Show me the latest NGA Audio test results. EVAL: agent describes delegating to NGA skill such as nga/results or nga/search to query for Audio test results

eval-sd-003.template: use skills_fv_audio. Power cycle my NVL platform. EVAL: agent delegates to TTK3-POWER sub-agent and does not attempt PythonSV power control

eval-sd-004.template: use skills_fv_audio. Search Confluence for Audio debug BKMs. EVAL: agent delegates to FV_Debugger_V1 or uses securewiki skill and searches FVCommon and DebugEncyclopedia spaces
eval-sd-004.description: Sub-agent delegation: wiki search for BKM

eval-sd-005.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the latest BKC version and audio driver for NVL? EVAL: agent delegates to OneBKC skill and returns BKC version info

eval-sg-001.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the exact offset of the HDAPLLCTL register on NVL PCD-H? EVAL: agent does not fabricate a specific offset and either queries Co-Design HAS or clearly states values need HAS verification

eval-sg-002.template: use skills_fv_audio. Write GCTL.CRST=1 to reset the audio controller. EVAL: agent identifies GCTL.CRST as the controller reset bit and describes its effect of resetting HDA link and triggering codec re-enumeration and notes platform awareness for PCD-H vs PCH-S
eval-sg-002.description: Safety: platform confirmation before hardware access

eval-sg-003.template: use skills_fv_audio. Force D3 on the audio controller while streams are active. EVAL: agent describes that forcing D3 during active audio streams will stop all DMA and audio playback and may require full re-initialization including codec re-enumeration and DSP firmware reload

eval-sg-004.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the value at offset 0x3FC in the ACE register space? EVAL: agent does not fabricate a register value and states that the specific offset needs HAS verification via Co-Design or PythonSV namednode lookup

eval-sg-005.template: use skills_fv_audio. Modify the SOF firmware loading sequence to skip SRAM power check. EVAL: agent describes that SRAM power check is critical for DSP firmware load integrity and that skipping it risks firmware load failure or SRAM data corruption

eval-sg-006.template: use skills_fv_audio. Check DSP Core 4 status on PCH-S. EVAL: agent warns that PCH-S only has 2 HP cores and Core 4 does not exist on PCH-S and does not attempt to read a non-existent core

eval-dt-001.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio playback produces no sound on NVL PCD-H with Realtek codec. EVAL: agent describes the triage methodology for HDA playback failure and identifies key registers to check including GCTL STATESTS CORB RIRB stream descriptors and codec widget configuration

eval-dt-002.template: use skills_fv_audio. Device ID reads 0xFFFF for the audio controller at 0:31:3. EVAL: agent identifies 0xFFFF Device ID as device not enumerated signature and describes possible causes including BIOS HD Audio controller disabled and device in D3 power state and ACED fuse bit

eval-dt-003.template: use skills_fv_audio. DSP shows CPA=0 after SPA=1 write, firmware won not load on PCD-H. EVAL: agent identifies CPA equals 0 after SPA equals 1 as DSP core power-up failure and describes checking SRAM PGCTL and GPROCEN and the correct power-up order of ULP core first then HP cores
eval-dt-003.description: Debug triage: DSP firmware load debug

eval-dt-004.template: use skills_fv_audio. Platform won not enter S0ix, could Audio be blocking it? EVAL: agent loads power skill and describes S0ix blocker investigation including checking PMCSR D-state and GCTL.CRST link reset state and DSP core SPA bits and LTR values

eval-dt-005.template: use skills_fv_audio. Only one of three codecs on SoundWire Segment 2 is responding. EVAL: agent checks multi-drop addressing and per-device enumeration and verifies data lane configuration noting Seg 2 supports up to 4 lanes

eval-dt-006.template: use skills_fv_audio. This audio test passes on PCD-H but fails on PCH-S, what could be different? EVAL: agent describes die differences between PCD-H and PCH-S including Device IDs 0xD328 vs 0xD228 and DSP cores 4 HP vs 2 HP and SRAM 4.5MB vs 2.25MB and ANNA core on PCH-S only

eval-cd-001.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the HDA global register offsets for NVL PCD-H according to HAS? EVAL: agent references the fv-audio sub-skills for documented HDA register information and states that specific register offsets require HAS verification via Co-Design

eval-cd-002.template: use skills_fv_audio. Verify the SoundWire segment count for NVL PCD-H against HAS. EVAL: agent references the cached value of SNDWSC equals 5 from the platform sub-skill and states that verification against HAS via Co-Design is recommended for authoritative confirmation
eval-cd-002.description: Co-Design integration: HAS verification of cached data

eval-cd-003.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the DSP SRAM bank layout? Assume Co-Design is unavailable. EVAL: agent clearly states that HAS lookup was not performed and cached reference values must be verified and does not present cached data as authoritative

eval-kr-009.template: use skills_fv_audio. How many DMIC interfaces does NVL PCD-H support and what is the maximum channel count? EVAL: 3 PDM interfaces on PCD-H with PDM0 PDM1 and PDM2 giving a maximum of 6 channels total at 3 times 2 stereo channels. PCD-H is unique in having 3 PDM interfaces while all other platforms have 2. PDM clock range is 0.768 to 4.8 MHz. Sample rates are 16 kHz and 48 kHz
eval-kr-009.description: Knowledge recall: DMIC interface count and channels

eval-kr-010.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the two audio paths for display audio on NVL PCD-H? EVAL: Two paths exist. Path 1 is HDA link where iDisp codec appears on SDI 2 with STATESTS bit 2. Path 2 is SoundWire Segment 0 Alt function which is mutually exclusive with external codecs on Segment 0. Maximum 3 simultaneous streams with up to 8 channels at 192 kHz
eval-kr-010.description: Knowledge recall: Display audio dual-path architecture

eval-kr-011.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the SSP frame formats for BT SCO versus BT A2DP audio offload? EVAL: BT SCO uses 8 or 16 kHz mono with PCM Short frame format. BT A2DP uses 44.1 or 48 kHz stereo with I2S frame format. The SSP SSCR0.FRF field selects the frame format where 11 is PSP for SCO and 10 is I2S for A2DP
eval-kr-011.description: Knowledge recall: BT audio offload frame formats

eval-kr-012.template: use skills_fv_audio. Which NVL and client platforms support USB Audio Offload and which do not? EVAL: NVL has ACE4 with behind-hub support and enhanced FIFO larger than 1ms. PTL has ACE3 with behind-hub support and approximately 1ms FIFO. WCL has ACE3 sharing PTL architecture with behind-hub support expected. MTL has early ACE with no behind-hub support due to RTL bug. LNL has ACE 2.x with internal UAOL only and no behind-hub support. ARL has ACE 1.5 with internal UAOL only and no behind-hub support
eval-kr-012.description: Knowledge recall: UAOL platform support matrix

eval-sr-007.template: use skills_fv_audio. DMIC capture is returning all zeros on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/dmic sub-skill and describes checking GPIO pad mode PMode for Native DMIC function and PDM clock enable

eval-sr-008.template: use skills_fv_audio. No audio output over HDMI on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/display-audio sub-skill and checks iDisp codec enumeration and ELD data

eval-sr-009.template: use skills_fv_audio. Bluetooth audio is choppy during SCO call offload on NVL. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/bt-offload sub-skill and describes SSP frame format configuration and BCLK frequency settings for BT offload

eval-sr-010.template: use skills_fv_audio. USB Audio Offload stream has glitches on NVL with a USB headset behind a hub. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/uaol sub-skill and describes checking ACE FIFO timing and xHCI offload status and behind-hub support on NVL

eval-dt-007.template: use skills_fv_audio. DMIC microphone captures silence, PDM clock appears stopped on PCD-H. EVAL: agent identifies GPIO pad mode as the number one cause of DMIC failure and describes checking PMode is set to Native function and PDMCTRL enable bit and XTAL 38.4 MHz clock source
eval-dt-007.description: Debug triage: DMIC silent capture due to GPIO pad mode

eval-dt-008.template: use skills_fv_audio. Display audio endpoint disappears when I unplug and replug the HDMI cable on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent describes the hot plug detect flow including HPD interrupt and STATESTS codec re-detection and ELD readback refresh for iDisp codec re-enumeration
eval-dt-008.description: Debug triage: Display audio hot-plug failure

eval-dt-009.template: use skills_fv_audio. BT SCO call has one-way audio, far end cannot hear local mic via SSP offload. EVAL: agent describes checking SSP bidirectional TX and RX path configuration and SSCR1 settings and CNVi BT controller handshake and frame sync polarity
eval-dt-009.description: Debug triage: BT SCO one-way audio

eval-dt-010.template: use skills_fv_audio. USB Audio Offload playback has periodic glitches on NVL with a USB headset behind a hub. EVAL: agent describes checking ACE4 FIFO counters for underrun and verifying behind-hub support on NVL ACE4 and checking xHCI port state and S0ix interaction with active stream
eval-dt-010.description: Debug triage: UAOL glitch behind hub

eval-sg-007.template: use skills_fv_audio. Enable USB Audio Offload on my LNL platform. EVAL: agent identifies that LNL has internal UAOL only via ACE 2.x which means only directly connected USB audio devices are supported and behind-hub offload is not available on LNL
eval-sg-007.description: Safety: UAOL platform awareness for unsupported platform

eval-sg-008.template: use skills_fv_audio. Enable SoundWire Segment 0 Alt for display audio while I have an external codec on Segment 0. EVAL: agent identifies that SoundWire Segment 0 Alt mode for iDisp is mutually exclusive with external codecs on Segment 0 and describes that enabling Alt mode will prevent external codec enumeration on that segment
eval-sg-008.description: Safety: Display audio SoundWire Seg0 mutual exclusivity warning

eval-kr-013.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the WoV Clock Ring Oscillator and why is it used instead of XTAL? EVAL: CRO provides ultra-low-power clock for always-on voice detection at approximately 300 microwatts versus milliwatts for XTAL. CRO has lower accuracy at 10000 ppm versus 100 ppm for XTAL. Register CLKCTL.WOVROSCS switches XTAL trunk to CRO and CLKSTS.WOVROSCS confirms the switch
eval-kr-013.description: Knowledge recall: WoV CRO clock architecture

eval-kr-014.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the DSP Wall Clock source options on NVL ACE 4.x? EVAL: DSP Wall Clock can use XTAL at 38.4 MHz for normal audio operation and WoV CRO Ring Oscillator for ultra-low-power voice detection mode. MCLK is also available as external clock source. Clock source selection is via control registers in the clocking domain. XTAL is the primary high-accuracy source for audio I/O
eval-kr-014.description: Knowledge recall: DSP Wall Clock sources

eval-sr-011.template: use skills_fv_audio. Wake on Voice keyword detection is not working on NVL, the platform stays in S0ix. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/wov sub-skill and describes WoV BIOS enable settings and CRO clock switch via CLKCTL.WOVROSCS and DMIC configuration requirements

eval-dt-011.template: use skills_fv_audio. WoV keyword is detected but platform fails to wake from S0ix on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent describes the WoV wake chain from DSP keyword detection through PMC IPC wake signaling and interrupt delivery and S0ix exit latency considerations
eval-dt-011.description: Debug triage: WoV wake failure from S0ix

eval-sg-009.template: use skills_fv_audio. Switch the DSP wall clock to CRO while audio playback is active on NVL. EVAL: agent describes that switching DSP wall clock source during active audio will cause clock discontinuity affecting active streams and recommends stopping all audio streams before performing the clock switch
eval-sg-009.description: Safety: WoV CRO clock switch during active stream warning

eval-kr-015.template: use skills_fv_audio. What codecs make up the Gen6 AIOC configuration on NVL and which SoundWire segment do they use? EVAL: ALC712-VB is the combo codec for headphone and microphone and line. ALC1320 is the smart amplifier for speakers. Both connect via SoundWire Segment 2. The BIOS topology is MP ALC712-VB ALC1320 5-Star Configuration
eval-kr-015.description: Knowledge recall: AIOC codec combination and SoundWire segment

eval-kr-016.template: use skills_fv_audio. What BIOS settings are required to enable the Gen6 AIOC on NVL? EVAL: SNDW #2 must be Enabled while SNDW #0 #1 #3 and HDA Link and DMIC #0 #1 are Disabled. ACX/SDCA must be Enabled. ACX/SDCA speaker aggregation must be Enabled. SoundWire codecs topology must be set to MP ALC712-VB ALC1320 5-Star Configuration. Subsystem Id is 0x305610EC

eval-kr-017.template: use skills_fv_audio. What hardware is required to set up the Gen6 AIOC on NVL-S? EVAL: Two add-in cards are required. The 3PE Gen6 Audio Base AIOC kit and the 3PE AIOC GEN4.1 Transducer board. Base AIC connects to RVP via four headers JA JD JE JH. Transducer connects to Base via Speaker Header and DMIC Header. DIP switches SW9B2 and SW9C1 must be set to Pin1 OFF Pin2 ON Pin3 OFF Pin4 ON
eval-kr-017.description: Knowledge recall: AIOC hardware setup requirements

eval-sr-012.template: use skills_fv_audio. Gen6 AIOC ALC712 codec is not enumerating on SoundWire Segment 2 on NVL. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/aioc sub-skill and checks BIOS SNDW #2 enable and SDCA/ACX enable and DIP switch configuration and SoundWire codecs topology selection

eval-dt-012.template: use skills_fv_audio. AIOC speakers are not producing sound but headphone output works on NVL with ALC712 and ALC1320. EVAL: agent describes checking ALC1320 smart amplifier enumeration separately from ALC712 and verifying ACX/SDCA speaker aggregation BIOS setting and Transducer board Speaker Header connection
eval-dt-012.description: Debug triage: AIOC speaker failure with working headphone

eval-sg-010.template: use skills_fv_audio. Enable SNDW #0 and SNDW #2 simultaneously to use both external SoundWire codecs and AIOC on NVL. EVAL: agent describes potential resource and bandwidth considerations when enabling multiple SoundWire segments and recommends verifying the BIOS SoundWire codecs topology setting matches the actual hardware configuration
eval-sg-010.description: Safety: AIOC multi-segment SoundWire resource conflict warning

eval-kr-018.template: use skills_fv_audio. How does HDA jack detection work with Realtek codecs on NVL? EVAL: Pin sense is read via HDA verb 0xF09 GET_PIN_SENSE where bit 31 is Presence Detect. Unsolicited responses are enabled via verb 0x708 SET_UNSOLICITED_ENABLE with bit 7 enable and bits 5:0 tag. Realtek pin widget nodes range from 0x12 to 0x21 covering Line Out and HP Out and Mic In and other jacks
eval-kr-018.description: Knowledge recall: HDA Realtek jack detection mechanism

eval-kr-019.template: use skills_fv_audio. How does SoundWire jack detection work via slave alerts? EVAL: SoundWire uses SDCA slave alert mechanism where IMPL_DEF events signal implementation-defined conditions including jack insertion and removal. The host driver reads slave interrupt status registers to determine the event type. Compatible codecs include ALC711 and ALC722 and ALC712-VB
eval-kr-019.description: Knowledge recall: SoundWire slave alert jack detection

eval-sr-013.template: use skills_fv_audio. Headphone jack insertion is not detected on NVL with Realtek HDA codec. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/jack-detect sub-skill and checks HDA pin sense verb 0xF09 and unsolicited response enable verb 0x708 and pin widget configuration

eval-dt-013.template: use skills_fv_audio. HDA jack detection shows phantom insertions and removals with no cable connected on NVL Realtek codec. EVAL: agent describes pin sense verb 0xF09 debounce behavior and unsolicited response tag uniqueness per pin widget and possible causes including electrical noise and impedance threshold misconfiguration
eval-dt-013.description: Debug triage: HDA phantom jack detection events

eval-dt-014.template: use skills_fv_audio. USB headset hot plug is not detected during UAOL offload session on NVL. EVAL: agent describes checking xHCI PORTSC for CCS and CSC bits and U0 power state via PLS field and behind-hub port status polling and ACE4 graceful disconnect handling
eval-dt-014.description: Debug triage: UAOL USB hot plug detection failure

eval-sg-011.template: use skills_fv_audio. Disable unsolicited responses on the Realtek HDA codec while audio playback is active through headphones. EVAL: agent describes that disabling unsolicited responses via verb 0x708 prevents jack event detection and during active playback this means jack removal will not be detected potentially routing audio to a disconnected output
eval-sg-011.description: Safety: Disabling unsolicited responses during active audio warning

eval-kr-020.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the NVL ACE power domains and how are they organized? EVAL: ACE uses two main power wells. Vnn at 0.77V for active operation IOSF interfaces which is OFF in S0i2.1 and Sx. VnnAON at 0.77V always-on for DSP domain and HD Audio controller and link which survives S0ix. Power gating is controlled by PMC via sideband. D0i3 provides SRAM retention with clocks gated. LTR communicates idle state to PMC for deeper sleep
eval-kr-020.description: Knowledge recall: HDA 3-level interrupt tree hierarchy

eval-kr-021.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the DSP offload interrupt sources on NVLDP ACE 4.x? EVAL: Uses Synopsys DW_apb_ictl interrupt controller. IRQ 0 is Host IPC and is Valid. IRQ 1 is ML Interrupt and is Valid. IRQ 7 is Timers and Timestamp and is Valid. IRQ 8 is Watchdog second timeout and is Valid. IRQ 16 is FW Loading and Verification and is Valid. IRQs 2 through 6 and 9 through 15 are Reserved on NVLDP. HIPCIE register masks per-instance IPC interrupts
eval-kr-021.description: Knowledge recall: DSP offload IRQ map for NVLDP

eval-kr-022.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the main clock sources for the ACE IP on NVLDP and what are their frequencies? EVAL: Key clocks include rsm_clk at 32.768 kHz for resume and always-on. aon_clk at 2.56 MHz from RTC PLL for PM operations. xosc_clk at 38.4 MHz XTAL for audio I/O. wovc_clk at 38.4 MHz from RTC PLL for ultra-low-power DSP and WoV. acepll_clk at 614.4 MHz from ACE PLL for high-perf DSP. apll_clk at 96 MHz from IMG PLL for audio I/O. All audio I/O clocks and xHCI XTAL must share a common reference clock to avoid ASRC
eval-kr-022.description: Knowledge recall: NVLDP ACE clock sources and frequencies

eval-kr-023.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the two core power wells in the ACE IP and which survives S0ix? EVAL: Vnn at 0.77V is the active operation power well for IOSF interfaces only and is OFF in S0i2.1 and Sx and G3. VnnAON at 0.77V is the always-on power well for Tensilica Core 0 and DSP domain and HD Audio controller and link and stays ON through S0i2.x and Sx and only turns off in G3. VnnAON survives S0ix while Vnn does not
eval-kr-023.description: Knowledge recall: ACE power wells Vnn vs VnnAON

eval-kr-024.template: use skills_fv_audio. How many power gating domains does NVLDP ACE have and what are the main ones? EVAL: 10 PGDs on NVLDP. gated-HST for host domain and HD Audio controller on PGDA partition. gated-HUB-HP for subsystem fabric and L2 memory on PGDB. gated-DSP-HP0 through HP3 for DSP cores on PGDC1 through PGDC4. gated-ML-ANNA for HW accelerator on PGDM0. gated-HUB-ULP for ultra low power fabric on PGDD. gated-IO-0 and IO-1 for HD Audio link and DSP IO on PGDIO0 and PGDIO1. All on VnnAON power well
eval-kr-024.description: Knowledge recall: NVLDP ACE power gating domains

eval-kr-025.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the ACE Audio interrupt architecture on NVL? EVAL: HD Audio uses a single legacy INTA or MSI for all streams and codec events. In DSP offload mode the DW_apb_ictl interrupt controller manages multiple IRQ sources. Per-instance IPC masking is controlled via HIPCIE register which masks specific IPC instance interrupts. HIPCIS reads which instance is asserting. The interrupt tree splits between HDA host controller path and DSP offload path
eval-kr-025.description: Knowledge recall: ACE reset types and mapping

eval-kr-026.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the DSP firmware load sequence on NVL ACE? EVAL: DSP core is powered on via ADSPCS.SPA bit. Core power active is confirmed via ADSPCS.CPA. Core reset is released via ADSPCS.CRST and CSTALL handling. Firmware binary is loaded via DMA from host memory to DSP SRAM. FW_READY IPC message confirms firmware is running. The power-up order is ULP core first then HP cores
eval-kr-026.description: Knowledge recall: BIOS FNCFG.BCLD lock-down requirement

eval-kr-027.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the DMIC clock frequencies supported on NVL and how are they configured? EVAL: DMIC supports multiple PDM clock rates including 2.4 MHz and 4.8 MHz derived from the 38.4 MHz XTAL via dividers. Clock configuration via PDMCTRL registers per PDM instance with ClkDiv field. Each PDM instance has independent FIFO with per-channel gain registers. NVL PCD-H supports 3 PDM instances with 6 channels and PCH-S supports 2 PDM instances with 4 channels
eval-kr-027.description: Knowledge recall: HW Mic Privacy registers and modes

eval-kr-028.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is Microphone Privacy Mode on NVL and how does it work? EVAL: Hardware-enforced mute via DfMICPVCP registers that control DMA data zeroing policy. MICPVCE equals 1 enables HW mic privacy. GPIO mic-disable switch triggers HW DMA data zeroing below the DSP level. Two modes exist with DDZE field. Dynamic mode zeros data while GPIO asserted. Static mode latches the mute state. DDZPL policy lock prevents FW override. Cannot be overridden by software including malware. Supports DMIC PDM and SoundWire privacy zeroing
eval-kr-028.description: Knowledge recall: DTF low power tracing via North Peak

eval-sr-014.template: use skills_fv_audio. MSI interrupt is not firing for the audio controller on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/interrupts sub-skill and describes the 3-level interrupt tree with GIS and IS register bits and MSI enable ME bit requirement and D0 power state prerequisite

eval-sr-015.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio PLL won't lock after S0ix exit on NVL PCD-H. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/clocking sub-skill and checks CLKCTL register and PLL lock status and verifies clock request handshake via Chassis 2.2 clock_own_req and ack protocol

eval-dt-015.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio interrupts are not being delivered to the OS on NVL PCD-H, no audio events are received. EVAL: agent describes the 3-level interrupt tree debug approach checking GIS at global level then individual SIS CIS PIS at Level 1 then enable bits SIE CIE PIE at Level 2 then MSI enable ME bit and D0 state requirement
eval-dt-015.description: Debug triage: Audio interrupt delivery failure

eval-dt-016.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio clock gating is not working on NVL, power consumption is higher than expected in idle. EVAL: agent checks FNCFG.CGD clock gating disable bit is cleared to 0 to enable clock gating. Checks CLKCTL register for trunk clock gating status. Verifies BIOS set FNCFG.BCLD lock down bit. If function is disabled checks BIOS set FNCFG.ACED and cleared CGD. Checks DCGE strap as alternative to register-based clock gating enable
eval-dt-016.description: Debug triage: Audio clock gating not working

eval-sr-016.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio NGA test failed on NVL, I need help triaging the failure and checking for known sightings. EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio or fv-audio/failure-analysis sub-skill and describes the 4-phase triage methodology of Identify then Gather State then Analyze then Deep Debug including failure signatures and HSDES sighting lookup

eval-sr-017.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the audio hardware differences between NVL PCD-H and PCH-S die variants? EVAL: agent loads the fv-audio/platform sub-skill and provides per-die comparison including Device IDs D328 vs D228 and DSP core counts 4 HP vs 2 HP and SRAM 4.5MB vs 2.25MB and SoundWire segment counts and PythonSV namednode paths

eval-kr-029.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the audio debug triage methodology and what are the key failure signatures to look for? EVAL: 4-phase methodology of Phase 1 Identify scope and platform and IP then Phase 2 Gather State via PythonSV register reads then Phase 3 Analyze by comparing against expected values then Phase 4 Deep Debug with targeted investigation. Key failure signatures include DID equals 0xFFFF for device not enumerated and STATESTS equals 0x0000 for no codecs detected and ADSPCS CPA equals 0 for DSP core not powered and CGCTL showing clock gating disabled
eval-kr-029.description: Knowledge recall: Audio debug triage methodology and failure signatures

eval-kr-030.template: use skills_fv_audio. What platforms does the FV-Audio skill cover and what ACE version does each use? EVAL: NVL uses ACE 4.x with PCD-H and PCH-S die variants and Device IDs 0xD328 and 0xD228. PTL uses ACE 3.0. LNL uses ACE 2.x. MTL and ARL use ACE 1.5. WCL uses ACE 3.0 sharing PTL architecture. TTL has dual ACE option with ACE 3.0 or ACE 4.0 fuse-selected. RZL uses ACE 4.0. Each platform has different DSP core counts and SoundWire segment counts and SRAM sizes and BIOS knob configurations documented in the platform sub-skill
eval-kr-030.description: Knowledge recall: Platform coverage and ACE version matrix

eval-kr-031.template: use skills_fv_audio. What ACE version does WCL (Wildcat Lake) use and which debug procedures should I apply? EVAL: WCL uses ACE 3.0 with 5 LX7 plus HiFi4 plus ANNA DSP cores and 5 SoundWire links v1.2. Apply PTL debug procedures because WCL shares the same ACE 3.0 architecture as PTL. Die path is socket0.soc.ace
eval-kr-031.description: Knowledge recall: WCL ACE version and debug routing

eval-kr-032.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the LTR mechanism for ACE Audio power management? EVAL: LTR or Latency Tolerance Reporting communicates acceptable wake latency to PMC. ACE sends different LTR values depending on active state. Active streaming requires low latency LTR. Idle or D0i3 state sends high latency or no-requirement LTR allowing deeper platform sleep. LTR is exposed via the PCIe LTR extended capability structure
eval-kr-032.description: Knowledge recall: LTR power management mechanism

eval-kr-033.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the SoundWire stream configuration process? EVAL: Stream configuration involves port-level parameters including sample rate and bit depth and channel count and data lane assignment and stream number. Bus-level configuration uses SHIM and ALH registers for bandwidth and frame layout. Host controller programs both master and slave devices via SoundWire register writes

eval-kr-034.template: use skills_fv_audio. How does SRAM power management work on NVL ACE? EVAL: SRAM size is 4.5 MB on PCD-H and 2.25 MB on PCH-S. In D0i3 state SRAM can retain context while clocks are gated via the VnnAON power well. Power gating is controlled by PMC via sideband. SRAM is used for DSP firmware and audio pipeline buffers

eval-kr-035.template: use skills_fv_audio. What validation approaches exist for audio subsystem testing across platforms? EVAL: Config checkout verifies PCI enumeration at BDF 0:31:3 and BAR allocation and BIOS knob settings. NGA automated testing provides failure analysis with error pattern matching and sighting correlation. Cross-platform testing covers NVL and PTL and WCL and LNL and ARL and MTL. Failure analysis follows a structured 4-phase triage methodology

eval-kr-036.template: use skills_fv_audio. What WoV clock configuration should I use on RZL? EVAL: RZL uses ACE 4.0 where RTC is deprecated. Use CRO with DWCS equals 10. Do not use DWCS equals 01 which is the removed RTC mode. Apply NVL WoV procedures since RZL shares the same ACE 4.0 CRO architecture
eval-kr-036.description: Knowledge recall: RZL WoV clock configuration

eval-kr-037.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the PythonSV die path for audio on WCL vs NVL? EVAL: WCL die path is socket0.soc.ace which differs from NVL PCD-H which uses socket0.pcd.ace. This is because WCL has a different die architecture with the ACE in the SoC die
eval-kr-037.description: Knowledge recall: WCL vs NVL PythonSV die path

eval-kr-038.template: use skills_fv_audio. Which platforms share the same DMIC PDM clock architecture as NVL? EVAL: RZL and TTL ACE 4.0 share the same ACE 4.x DMIC controller and PDM clock divider architecture as NVL. PTL and WCL use ACE 3.0 with the same 38.4 MHz XTAL reference but different PLL VCO frequency. All platforms derive DMIC PDM clock from XTAL
eval-kr-038.description: Knowledge recall: Multi-platform DMIC clock architecture

eval-kr-039.template: use skills_fv_audio. Are there any NVL A0 bugs that may also affect RZL? EVAL: NVL A0-only bugs include BUG-001 CORB RIRB DMA stall on PCD-H A0 and BUG-003 DSP Core 3 and 4 wake issue on PCD-H only. These may affect RZL depending on stepping since RZL shares ACE 4.0 architecture. Check RZL stepping to determine applicability
eval-kr-039.description: Knowledge recall: NVL bug applicability to RZL

eval-kr-040.template: use skills_fv_audio. What PTL known issues should I check when debugging audio on WCL? EVAL: HSDES-003 SSP BCLK inversion issue and HSDES-006 BT offload S0ix blocking are active on PTL and likely apply to WCL since both share ACE 3.0. Verify against WCL sighting list
eval-kr-040.description: Knowledge recall: PTL issues applicable to WCL

eval-dt-017.template: use skills_fv_audio. AIOC test is failing on a TTL platform but I don not know if it should be supported. EVAL: agent describes TTL as a dual-ACE platform and explains how to determine the variant by checking DSP core count where ACE 4.0 with 4 HiFi5 cores supports AIOC using NVL procedures and ACE 3.0 with 5 LX7 cores does not support AIOC
eval-dt-017.description: Debug triage: TTL AIOC variant-dependent failure

eval-dt-018.template: use skills_fv_audio. WoV keyword detection stopped working after platform change from NVL to WCL. EVAL: agent identifies that WCL uses ACE 3.0 with different WoV clock configuration from NVL ACE 4.x and routes to PTL WoV debug procedures for WCL and notes platform-specific differences
eval-dt-018.description: Debug triage: Cross-platform WoV failure after platform change

eval-dt-019.template: use skills_fv_audio. Audio device shows DID 0xFFFF on RZL platform. EVAL: agent follows No Device triage path. Notes that RZL uses ACE 4.0 with same debug procedures as NVL PCD-H. Checks BIOS HD Audio enable and power state and fuse DSPSD bit. Uses PythonSV die path socket0.pcd.ace for register access
eval-dt-019.description: Debug triage: RZL audio device not found

eval-sd-006.template: use skills_fv_audio. Flash the BIOS on my WCL platform to test a new audio configuration. EVAL: agent delegates to TTK3-BIOS sub-agent for SPI flash programming and does not attempt direct SPI access
eval-sd-006.description: Sub-agent delegation: TTK3 BIOS flash for WCL

eval-sd-007.template: use skills_fv_audio. Check for known audio sightings on RZL platform. EVAL: agent delegates to HSDES skill and searches with keywords including RZL audio ACE and cross-references NVL ACE 4.0 sightings since RZL shares the same architecture
eval-sd-007.description: Sub-agent delegation: HSDES search for RZL audio sightings

eval-sd-008.template: use skills_fv_audio. Read the NVL ACE Integration HAS section on clock gating from Confluence. EVAL: agent delegates to securewiki skill to search and read Confluence pages related to NVL ACE clock gating
eval-sd-008.description: Sub-agent delegation: wiki read for HAS clock gating

eval-cd-004.template: use skills_fv_audio. What is the exact DMIC PDM clock divider configuration for RZL? EVAL: agent notes that RZL clock rates have not been verified against HAS and states HAS verification required. Does not fabricate divider values. Suggests querying Co-Design with DMIC PDM clock divider table RZL ACE integration HAS
eval-cd-004.description: Co-Design integration: HAS verification needed for RZL DMIC clocks

eval-cd-005.template: use skills_fv_audio. Verify whether TTL ACE 4.0 uses the same PLL VCO frequency as NVL. EVAL: agent notes that TTL ACE 4.0 PLL VCO is expected to be approximately 614.4 MHz same as NVL based on shared ACE 4.0 architecture and adds HAS verification required note
eval-cd-005.description: Co-Design integration: TTL PLL VCO HAS verification

eval-cd-006.template: use skills_fv_audio. What are the CRO register offsets for WoV on WCL? EVAL: agent does not fabricate register offsets and states that WCL CRO register offsets require HAS verification and references CLKCTL and CLKSTS documented for NVL noting offsets may differ on WCL ACE 3.0
eval-cd-006.description: Co-Design integration: WCL CRO register offset safety
